excerpt: Paraphrase: Paul addresses the church of God that is in Corinth.
Paraphrase: Paul rehearses the received proclamation that Christ died, was buried, was raised, and appeared.
Paraphrase: Paul states that he will remain in Ephesus until Pentecost.
Paraphrase: Peter addresses the elect exiles of the dispersion in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia.
Paraphrase: Peter blesses God for giving new birth into a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead.
Paraphrase: Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy address the church of the Thessalonians in God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
Paraphrase: Paul states that Jesus died and rose again and that God will bring with Jesus those who have died.
Paraphrase: Paul urges Timothy to remain on at Ephesus to charge certain persons not to teach differently.
Paraphrase: Paul addresses the church of God in Corinth together with all the saints in the whole of Achaia.
Paraphrase: Paul says that the one who raised the Lord Jesus will raise believers also with Jesus.
Paraphrase: Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy address the church of the Thessalonians in God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
Paraphrase: Paul says Demas has gone to Thessalonica, Crescens to Galatia, Titus to Dalmatia, and Tychicus has been sent to Ephesus.
Paraphrase: Paul asks Timothy to bring the cloak left with Carpus at Troas when he comes.
Paraphrase: Paul notes that Trophimus was left ill at Miletus.
Paraphrase: Cornelius of Caesarea sends for Peter, hears the message, and the Holy Spirit falls on Gentiles who are then baptized.
Paraphrase: Barnabas brings Saul to Antioch, where they teach with the church and the disciples are first called Christians.
Paraphrase: Barnabas and Saul sail to Cyprus, proclaim in Salamis, traverse the island to Paphos, and Sergius Paulus believes after Elymas is opposed.
Paraphrase: The Jerusalem council addresses whether Gentile believers must be circumcised and sends its decision in a letter.
Paraphrase: Paul comes to Lystra, where he finds Timothy, a disciple well spoken of by the believers there and in Iconium.
Paraphrase: The mission arrives at Philippi, a Roman colony, and Lydia responds in faith and hospitality.
Paraphrase: Paul and Silas proclaim in Thessalonica and some are persuaded, forming a new believing community.
Paraphrase: Paul and his companions minister in Berea where many receive the message eagerly.
Paraphrase: Paul reasons in Athens and speaks at the Areopagus, with some joining and believing.
Paraphrase: Dionysius the Areopagite, a woman named Damaris, and others join Paul and believe in Athens.
Paraphrase: Opponents in Thessalonica attack Jason's house and drag Jason before the city authorities.
Paraphrase: Paul ministers in Corinth with companions and a new community takes shape there.
Paraphrase: While Gallio is proconsul of Achaia, Paul is brought before the tribunal in Corinth and Sosthenes is beaten before it.
Paraphrase: Apollos arrives at Ephesus, is instructed more accurately by Priscilla and Aquila, and later assists believers in Achaia.
Paraphrase: Paul stays in Corinth with Titius Justus, whose house is next door to the synagogue.
Paraphrase: Crispus, the ruler of the synagogue in Corinth, believes in the Lord together with his household.
Paraphrase: Paul teaches in Ephesus and the message spreads broadly in the region.
Paraphrase: Demetrius the silversmith in Ephesus warns fellow craftsmen that Paul's message threatens their trade and Artemis devotion.
Paraphrase: The crowd in Ephesus seizes Aristarchus, Paul's Macedonian travel companion, in the uproar.
Paraphrase: In Ephesus, Alexander is pushed forward to address the crowd, but the assembly shouts in defense of Artemis.
Paraphrase: Paul withdraws to reason daily in the hall of Tyrannus in Ephesus.
Paraphrase: Paul goes by way of Assos, Mitylene, Chios, and Samos on the route toward Miletus.
Paraphrase: Sopater of Berea and Aristarchus and Secundus of Thessalonica are named among Paul's companions.
Paraphrase: Paul stays in Troas, departs by sea, and comes to Miletus where he summons the Ephesian elders.
Paraphrase: A prophet named Agabus comes down from Judea and warns that Paul will be bound in Jerusalem.
Paraphrase: Disciples from Caesarea bring Paul to lodge with Mnason of Cyprus, an early disciple.
Paraphrase: Jews in Jerusalem have previously seen Trophimus the Ephesian with Paul in the city.
Paraphrase: Paul lands at Tyre, finds disciples there, and continues onward on the journey to Jerusalem.
Paraphrase: Paul and his companions stay with Philip the evangelist in Caesarea, where his four daughters prophesy.
Paraphrase: Claudius Lysias writes to Felix, and Paul is brought under guard to Caesarea for a hearing before the governor.
Paraphrase: Tertullus presents the case against Paul before Felix in Caesarea on behalf of the Jerusalem leadership.
Paraphrase: Felix comes with Drusilla in Caesarea, hears Paul about faith in Christ, and keeps him in custody hoping for a bribe.
Paraphrase: Festus arrives in the province, goes from Caesarea to Jerusalem, and returns to Caesarea to hear Paul's case.
Paraphrase: Agrippa and Bernice come to Caesarea, where Festus presents Paul's case and a formal hearing is arranged.
Paraphrase: Paul is handed over to a centurion named Julius, and the party puts in at Sidon where Julius treats Paul kindly.
Paraphrase: Running under the lee of the small island called Clauda, the crew secures the ship's boat and undergirds the vessel.
Paraphrase: Paul voyage under guard touches Sidon, passes Cnidus, and reaches Fair Havens while debate continues about wintering at Phoenix.
Paraphrase: Paul comes to Myra in Lycia, then sails under Crete past Salmone and reaches Fair Havens.
Paraphrase: Paul is received on Malta and then sails onward by way of Syracuse, Rhegium, and Puteoli toward Rome.
Paraphrase: Believers come from Rome to meet Paul as far as Appii Forum and Three Taverns.
Paraphrase: Paul remains in Rome under guard while proclaiming the kingdom and teaching openly.
Paraphrase: Publius, the leading man of the island, receives Paul and his companions on Malta, and healings follow.
Paraphrase: Stephen is stoned outside Jerusalem after his witness before the council.
Paraphrase: Philip proclaims Christ in Samaria, many believe, and Peter and John pray for the new Samaritan believers.
Paraphrase: Saul encounter on the way to Damascus leads to his reversal, healing, and baptism.
Paraphrase: In Damascus, the disciple Ananias restores Saul's sight, addresses his calling, and baptizes him.
Paraphrase: In Lydda, Peter finds Aeneas bedridden for years and declares Jesus Christ has healed him.
Paraphrase: Peter ministers in the coastal plain and at Joppa, where many turn to the Lord after Tabitha is raised.
Paraphrase: In Joppa, the disciple Tabitha dies and is raised, and many in the city believe in the Lord.
Paraphrase: Peter stays many days in Joppa with Simon the tanner.
Paraphrase: Paul addresses the saints and faithful brothers and sisters in Christ in Colossae.
Paraphrase: Paul instructs that the letter be read in Laodicea and that the Colossians also read the letter from Laodicea.
Paraphrase: Onesimus, one of the Colossians, is sent with Tychicus and identified as a faithful and beloved brother.
Paraphrase: Paul addresses the saints in Ephesus, though the phrase 'in Ephesus' is textually debated.
Paraphrase: Paul addresses the churches of Galatia.
Paraphrase: Paul recounts Jerusalem recognition of his Gentile mission without imposing circumcision on Titus.
Paraphrase: James calls hearers to draw near to God, cleanse their hands, humble themselves, and repent before the Lord.
Paraphrase: John testifies about Jesus at Bethany beyond the Jordan where he was baptizing.
Paraphrase: Josephus describes John as a good man exhorting virtue and baptism.
Paraphrase: Josephus identifies Pontius Pilate as prefect governing Judea.
Paraphrase: Josephus describes the Pharisees, Sadducees, and Essenes as distinct schools among the Jews and gives an extended portrait of the Essenes.
Paraphrase: Josephus portrays the Zealots as a violent faction dominating Jerusalem during the war.
Paraphrase: John appears in the region around the Jordan proclaiming a baptism of repentance.
Paraphrase: Jesus comes into Galilee proclaiming God kingdom and calling for repentance and belief.
Paraphrase: Jesus calls Simon, Andrew, James, and John, and they leave their work to follow him.
Paraphrase: Jesus enters the temple precincts in Jerusalem and disrupts buying and selling.
Paraphrase: Pharisees and Herodians question Jesus in Jerusalem about paying taxes to Caesar.
Paraphrase: Pharisees go out and immediately conspire with the Herodians against Jesus after the Sabbath controversy.
Paraphrase: Jesus goes throughout Galilee teaching, proclaiming the kingdom, and healing.
Paraphrase: The crowds recognize that Jesus teaches with authority unlike the scribes.
Paraphrase: Paul and Timothy address all the saints in Christ Jesus who are in Philippi, with the overseers and deacons.
Paraphrase: Paul addresses Philemon, Apphia, Archippus, and the church in their house.
Paraphrase: John is told to write in a book and send it to the seven churches in Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, and Laodicea.
Paraphrase: John says that he was on the island called Patmos because of the word of God and the testimony of Jesus.
Paraphrase: Paul addresses all God beloved in Rome who are called to be saints.
Paraphrase: Paul says that he left Titus in Crete to set in order what remained and appoint elders in every town.
Paraphrase: Paul directs Titus to come to him at Nicopolis because he has decided to spend the winter there.
